finding.summary: The track shows healthy peak levels and typical kick-drum spectral balance, but the extreme silence fraction (0.88) dilutes RMS and overall level statistics. No problematic signs are evident from the isolated capture.
finding.probable_cause: The track contains very sparse hits (only 12% non-silent content), as designed.
finding.confidence_reason: The high silence fraction limits the reliability of RMS and integrated LUFS as indicators of average playback level, but the peak and spectral data from the hits are reliable.

evidence_refs.description: True peak at -5.5 dBFS, well below clipping.
88% of the capture is dead air, heavily skewing continuous level measurements.
Spectral balance dominated by 100 Hz (-18 dB), with energy falling off at higher frequencies, consistent with a typical kick drum.

proposal.reason: No corrective action is justified based on the isolated-track data. The track appears well-recorded with no clipping or spectral anomalies.
